title: Meriania glabra
intro: Meriania glabra is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "wild coffee" due to its caffeine-rich leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Meriania glabra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Meriania glabra include infusions and decoctions, which are traditionally used to treat fever, fatigue, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Meriania glabra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is a group of plants commonly known as the melastome or tropical sorrel family.
